user.title: Installation costs
user.description: Enter the financial details of your PV system. These values are the basis for all yield and amortization calculations.

data_description.installation_cost: Total cost of the PV system including hardware, installation, and any other one-time expenses. Used to calculate when the system breaks even.
data_description.commissioning_date: The date your PV system was first switched on. Used to calculate system age and the minimum history requirement for amortization projections.
data_description.statistics_start_date: The date from which this integration will read HA statistics. Set this to today if you are setting up the integration now. All values you enter on the next screen should reflect your totals up to and including this date.

history.title: Pre-tracking totals
history.description: Enter your cumulative financial totals as of the tracking start date. The integration will add any new earnings on top of these values. Leave at 0 if you have no prior data.

data.historical_savings: Electricity savings up to tracking start
data.historical_feed_in: Feed-in revenue up to tracking start

data_description.historical_savings: Total money saved through self-consumption up to your tracking start date. Check your own records or electricity bills.
data_description.historical_feed_in: Total feed-in revenue received up to your tracking start date. Check your utility statements or inverter portal.

entities.title: Energy sensors & pricing
entities.description: Select the sensors that provide your production and grid data, and choose how electricity prices and feed-in tariffs are sourced.

data_description.pv_production_entity: Sensor measuring the total energy your PV system has ever produced (kWh, state class: total_increasing). This is usually provided by your inverter integration.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor measuring the total energy exported to the grid (kWh, total_increasing). Self-consumption is calculated as production minus export.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Optional. Sensor measuring total energy drawn from the grid (kWh, total_increasing). Only required for the self-sufficiency sensor.
data_description.feed_in_tariff_mode: Fixed: you enter a static rate in ct/kWh on the next screen. Entity: you select a sensor whose current value is used as the tariff (useful for variable tariffs).
data_description.electricity_price_mode: Fixed: you enter a static price in ct/kWh on the next screen. Entity: you select a sensor whose value is used as the price (e.g. Tibber, Awattar).
data_description.rolling_window_days: How many recent days are used to calculate the average daily yield for projecting the break-even date. A longer window gives a smoother estimate. Default: 365.
data_description.has_battery: Enable if your PV system includes a battery. Additional steps will allow you to configure the battery sensors.

battery_type.title: Battery sensor type
battery_type.description: Select how your battery's charge and discharge data is available in Home Assistant.

data_description.battery_sensor_type: Bidirectional: one power sensor (W/kW) that is positive when charging and negative when discharging (or vice versa). Two sensors: separate kWh sensors for charge and discharge energy.

battery_sensors.title: Battery sensors
battery_sensors.description: Select the sensor(s) that provide battery charge and discharge data.

data_description.battery_power_entity: Sensor measuring battery power in W or kW. Positive or negative values indicate charging or discharging depending on your system.
data_description.battery_power_positive: Choose whether positive values from the power sensor indicate charging or discharging.
data_description.battery_charge_entity: Sensor measuring total energy charged into the battery (kWh, total_increasing).
data_description.battery_discharge_entity: Sensor measuring total energy discharged from the battery (kWh, total_increasing).

feed_in_tariff.title: Feed-in tariff
feed_in_tariff.description: Configure the rate at which exported energy is compensated.

data_description.feed_in_tariff_value: Your feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. Check your energy contract or grid operator confirmation.
data_description.feed_in_tariff_entity: Sensor providing the current feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. For accurate per-hour calculations, this sensor should have long-term statistics enabled.

electricity_price.title: Electricity price
electricity_price.description: Configure the price of electricity from the grid. This is used to calculate how much money you save by consuming your own solar power. {statistics_note}

data_description.electricity_price_value: Your electricity price in ct/kWh. Use the rate you pay for grid electricity, excluding any fixed fees.
data_description.electricity_price_entity: Sensor providing the current electricity price in ct/kWh. For accurate per-hour calculations, this sensor should have long-term statistics enabled.

error.installation_cost_zero: Installation cost must be greater than zero for amortization tracking.
error.statistics_before_commissioning: Tracking start date cannot be before the commissioning date.
error.duplicate_entity: This entity is already assigned to another measurement.
error.entity_not_available: Entity state is unknown or unavailable — cannot use as price source.
error.entity_not_numeric: Entity does not return a numeric value — cannot use as price source.

init.title: Installation costs
init.description: Edit the financial details of your PV system.

data_description.installation_cost: Total cost of the PV system including hardware, installation, and any other one-time expenses.
data_description.commissioning_date: The date your PV system was first switched on.
data_description.statistics_start_date: The date from which HA statistics are read. Changing this will shift which period the pre-tracking totals cover.

history.title: Pre-tracking totals
history.description: Edit your cumulative financial totals as of the tracking start date.

data.historical_savings: Electricity savings up to tracking start
data.historical_feed_in: Feed-in revenue up to tracking start

data_description.historical_savings: Total money saved through self-consumption up to your tracking start date.
data_description.historical_feed_in: Total feed-in revenue received up to your tracking start date.

entities.title: Energy sensors & pricing
entities.description: Edit sensors, pricing modes, and update settings.

data_description.pv_production_entity: Sensor measuring total energy produced by your PV system (kWh, total_increasing).
data_description.grid_export_entity: Sensor measuring total energy exported to the grid (kWh, total_increasing).
data_description.grid_import_entity: Optional. Required for the self-sufficiency sensor.
data_description.feed_in_tariff_mode: Fixed: static rate in ct/kWh. Entity: sensor-based tariff.
data_description.electricity_price_mode: Fixed: static price in ct/kWh. Entity: sensor-based price.
data_description.rolling_window_days: Recent days used for the break-even projection. Default: 365.
data_description.update_interval_minutes: How often the integration recalculates all values. Lower values increase recorder load.
data_description.has_battery: Enable if your PV system includes a battery.

battery_type.title: Battery sensor type
battery_type.description: Select how your battery's charge and discharge data is available in Home Assistant.

data_description.battery_sensor_type: Bidirectional: one power sensor (W/kW). Two sensors: separate kWh sensors for charge and discharge.

battery_sensors.title: Battery sensors
battery_sensors.description: Select the sensor(s) that provide battery charge and discharge data.

data_description.battery_power_entity: Sensor measuring battery power in W or kW.
data_description.battery_power_positive: Choose whether positive values indicate charging or discharging.
data_description.battery_charge_entity: Sensor measuring total energy charged into the battery (kWh, total_increasing).
data_description.battery_discharge_entity: Sensor measuring total energy discharged from the battery (kWh, total_increasing).

feed_in_tariff.title: Feed-in tariff
feed_in_tariff.description: Edit the rate at which exported energy is compensated.

data_description.feed_in_tariff_entity: Sensor providing the current feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. Should have long-term statistics enabled.

electricity_price.title: Electricity price
electricity_price.description: Edit the price of electricity from the grid. {statistics_note}

data_description.electricity_price_entity: Sensor providing the current electricity price in ct/kWh. Should have long-term statistics enabled.

error.installation_cost_zero: Installation cost must be greater than zero for amortization tracking.
error.statistics_before_commissioning: Tracking start date cannot be before the commissioning date.
error.duplicate_entity: This entity is already assigned to another measurement.
error.entity_not_available: Entity state is unknown or unavailable — cannot use as price source.
error.entity_not_numeric: Entity does not return a numeric value — cannot use as price source.

price_entity_no_statistics.title: Electricity price entity has no long-term statistics
price_entity_no_statistics.description: The entity `{entity_id}` is used as the electricity price source, but has no long-term statistics. The integration is falling back to the entity's current state for all calculations. Enable long-term statistics for this entity to improve accuracy.

tariff_entity_no_statistics.title: Feed-in tariff entity has no long-term statistics
tariff_entity_no_statistics.description: The entity `{entity_id}` is used as the feed-in tariff source, but has no long-term statistics. The integration is falling back to the entity's current state for all calculations. Enable long-term statistics for this entity to improve accuracy.
